subject: Mathematics
title: The Dichotomy Paradox
description: Learn The Dichotomy Paradox in one minute.

hook.text: To cross a room, you first go halfway. Then half of what's left. Then half again. You never arrive.

buildup.text: Zeno argued that motion requires completing infinitely many steps—an impossible task.

discovery.text: The sum 1/2 + 1/4 + 1/8 + 1/16 + ... equals exactly 1. Infinity resolves itself.

twist.text: Ancient Greeks had no concept of limits, so the paradox seemed genuinely unsolvable.

climax.text: Calculus formalized infinite sums and showed that many infinite processes have perfectly finite results.

punchline.text: You can take infinitely many steps and still arrive on time.
